---
title: 'Is it safe to use Credit Karma?'
date: '2018-04-01T06:34:00.0000000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'your Credit Crash Course'
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '6941491302464887634'
blogger_filename: '/2018/03/is-it-safe-to-use-credit-karma.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/your Credit Crash Course/feed.atom'
---

Yes!<br />
<br />
Credit Karma, Credit Sesame, and other identity theft protection or credit monitoring services are safe to use. They will not affect your credit score negatively in any way.<br />
<br />
The algorithms know to exclude checks by companies with these classifications.<br />
<br />
As far as "putting your information out there online", that's a matter of educating yourself about online security, such as passwords and locking devices such as phones.<br />
<br />
Your information is already online whether you access it or not. You want to use online wisdom, but it's safe to use, IMHO.<br />
<br />
Darrell Wolfe
